name: Sweet & Sour Stuffed Cabbage
recipeIngredient: 1 large head or 2 small heads cabbage
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Remove core from cabbage and cook in boiling water for 5 minutes until leaves become flexible.
3. Drain leaves carefully with tongs; you should end up with 15-18 nice leaves.
4. Spread drained leaves on paper towels and drain well.
5. Finely chop enough remaining cabbage to make 3 cups.
6. To make sauce, heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan.
7. Add half of chopped onions and cover; steam over low heat for 10 minutes.
8. Uncover and cook until tender but not browned; add tomatoes, tomato paste, salt, and pepper to taste.
9. Simmer until thickened (about 30 minutes). Stir in honey, lemon juice, raisins, apricots, and 1-1/2 cups chopped cabbage. Adjust seasonings for a balance of sweet and sour flavors.
10. To make filling, heat 1 tablespoon oil; toss with remaining onions. Cover and steam for 10 minutes. Uncover and cook until tender and browned.
11. In a large bowl, combine ground veal, beef, eggs, bread crumbs, and steamed onions.
12. Lay cabbage leaves rib side up on a flat surface; trim the tough rib using a sharp knife.
13. Place meat mixture onto the leaf, tuck in sides, and roll tightly to form a neat parcel.
14. Place seam-side down on the sauce in the baking dish; repeat until all ingredients are used.
15. Cover cabbage rolls with remaining sauce and bake for 2 hours.
Note: This can be prepared several days in advance and will improve in flavor.
